title: Cisco ISE Logs through Splunk Edge Processor — Pipeline Health and Routing

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(g) effectiveness measurement — pipeline-health is one input.
AU-4 (Audit Log Storage Capacity) — pipeline drops impact log fidelity.
compliance.controlObjective: Audit-log fidelity preservation when normalizing data through Edge Processor.
Logging-pipeline fidelity demonstrated end-to-end through the edge layer.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Misconfigure an Edge Processor pipeline regex causing 5% parse errors. UC-17.1.59 fires for that pipeline.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fix the regex. Drops return to 0. Alert clears.

dataSources: Splunk Edge Processor / Ingest Processor metrics from `index=_internal sourcetype=ep:metrics` (Splunk Edge Processor's self-telemetry). Pipeline-health fields: `pipeline_id`, `events_in`, `events_out`, `dropped_events`, `parse_errors`, `routing_decisions`. ISE-side context from `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` to confirm receipt.
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype="ep:metrics" earliest=-30m
| stats sum(events_in) as in, sum(events_out) as out, sum(dropped_events) as drops, sum(parse_errors) as parse_errors by pipeline_id
| eval drop_pct = round((drops/in)*100, 2)
| where drop_pct > 1 OR parse_errors > 0
description: Detects Splunk Edge Processor / Ingest Processor pipeline issues that affect ISE log ingestion — drops, parse errors, routing failures.
value: When Edge Processor is in the path between ISE and Splunk indexers, pipeline health is the missing link in observability. A 5% drop rate is invisible from the Splunk indexer side but obvious from EP metrics.
implementation: Confirm Edge Processor is sending self-telemetry to Splunk. Schedule every 15 min, search window `-30m@m to @m`.
visualization: (1) Per-pipeline drop-rate timechart. (2) Top-N parse errors. (3) Routing-decision distribution.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Initial pipeline deployment** — High parse errors during initial pipeline configuration; expected for first hour.
mitreAttack: N/A (ingest pipeline health — measures Splunk Edge Processor for ISE, no adversary technique)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Edge Processor or Ingest Processor deployed in path.
• Self-telemetry enabled.

Step 1 — Verify telemetry feed.
Step 2 — Search.
Step 3 — Provoke as above.
Step 4 — Dashboard with drop-rate per pipeline.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting: tune pipeline regex; verify EP capacity.
grandmaExplanation: ISE sends information to Splunk through a middleman. We watch the middleman to make sure it isn't dropping or breaking messages on the way through.
